name: Do replacement windows increase home value in these cities?

mainEntity.name: Do new windows meaningfully increase resale value or buyer appeal in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: Do new windows meaningfully increase resale value or buyer appeal in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: Replacement windows can improve a home’s marketability by making it look newer, quieter, and better maintained. In many cases, buyers respond strongly to windows that open smoothly, seal well, and show no signs of fogging or rot. In coastal San Mateo County, good windows may also signal that the home has been maintained against moisture and weather exposure. The financial return is not always a simple dollar-for-dollar payoff, though. Higher-end windows may not return their full cost on resale, but they can still add value through reduced maintenance, better comfort, and stronger buyer perception. The biggest payoff usually comes when the old windows are visibly failing or creating drafts and leaks. A well-installed, appropriately specified window system is more valuable than an expensive product that was installed poorly.
acceptedAnswer.description: New windows can improve curb appeal, energy performance, and buyer confidence, but the resale return depends on product quality, installation quality, and whether the existing windows were visibly failing. Buyers often value comfort and appearance as much as exact payback.
